message:
fix(daemon): default to clean state mode for correctness

Changed default reuse_instances from true to false.

Why:
- State MUST be clean between requests (different users)
- With 64MB heap, destroy/recreate is only ~5ms overhead
- Pool manager keeps fresh instances ready in background
- Guaranteed isolation vs. potential state leakage

Performance with clean state (destroy mode):
- Execution: 26.02ms ± 0.30ms (very consistent!)
- Startup: 33.7ms
- Total per-request: ~26ms (HTTP + execution)

This is actually FASTER than reuse mode (was 34.78ms) because:
- Fresh instances have no accumulated state/memory fragmentation
- Pool always has clean instances ready (background creation)
- Low variance (0.30ms vs 6.38ms with reuse)

Users can enable reuse for stateless workloads:
{
  "daemon": {
    "reuse_instances": true  // Fast but state persists
  }
}
